channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19550] crash in ldiskfs_clear_inode->__dquot_drop
item.description:
I've hit several crashes on my home MDS while trying to run "lipe_find3 MDTDEV -collect-fsize-stats". I've upgraded from kernel 4.18.0-477.27.1.el8_lustre.ddn17.x86_64 to 4.18.0-553.16.1.el8_lustre.ddn17.x86_64, and Lustre 2.14.0-ddn144 to 2.14.0-ddn167 and hit the same crash each time:

Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.099142] BUG: unable to handle kernel paging
 request at 0000100000000064
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.099206] Oops: 0000 [#1] SMP PTI
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.099222] CPU: 0 PID: 53 Comm: kswapd0 Tainte
d: G           OE     -------- -  - 4.18.0-553.16.1.el8_lustre.ddn17.x86_64 #1
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.099261] Hardware name: Dell Inc. Dell XPS42
0                  /0TP406, BIOS A04 03/27/2008
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.099289] RIP: 0010:dqput.part.18+0x3e/0x1e0
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.099586] Call Trace:
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.099721]  __dquot_drop+0x76/0x90
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.099739]  ldiskfs_clear_inode+0x1e/0x80 [ldi
skfs]
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.099812]  ldiskfs_evict_inode+0x65/0x660 [ldiskfs]
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.099854]  evict+0xd2/0x1a0
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.099870]  dispose_list+0x48/0x70
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.099885]  prune_icache_sb+0x52/0x80
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.100457]  super_cache_scan+0x123/0x1b0
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.100770]  do_shrink_slab+0x11d/0x330
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.101051]  shrink_slab+0xbe/0x2f0
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.101324]  shrink_node+0x257/0x710
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.101585]  balance_pgdat+0x2d7/0x550
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.101833]  kswapd+0x20b/0x3d0
Aug 29 18:27:33 192.168.20.1  [ 3026.102555]  kthread+0x134/0x150

It is possible that this is triggered by some unusual inode contents in my (very) old filesystem that is unlikely to be hit by a customer, but it seems prudent to test this with a non-trivial configuration (e.g. on Soak).

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
I hit this crash again, but this time with "lfs find /myth -type f -ost 3 --stripe-count 1 -skip 86 -print0 | xargs -0 -n1 -P 2 lfs migrate -i 2" to migrate 14% of my OST0003 to OST0002 to balance out the space, so lipe_find3 was not involved at all in this case, and it rules out bad interactions between ldiskfs and cached blocks accessed via the block device.

This appears to be either an ldiskfs or kernel inode cache bug or similar. I've run e2fsck on this filesystem many times, but need to capture a crashdump to understand what is causing this issue.

vkuznetsov, this is a crash in the kernel ldiskfs code, so it shouldn't matter what lipe_find3 is doing. It is just reading blocks from the disk. In the most recent case, I was running a very simple "lipe_find3 /dev/OSTDEV -print". So I don't think this is a lipe_find3 bug that you should work on, but an ldiskfs kernel bug that needs someone like Alex Z or Dongyang (unless you are very keen to learn ldiskfs code).
Old lipe version have same crashes? (For example, if we request the attributes in JSON format without filters?)

+1 when running lipe_find3 on the OST again (same bad address).

It is a bit strange, because lipe_find3 isn't interacting with ldiskfs in the kernel directly at all, it is just reading the blocks from the block device and using libext2fs in userspace to interpret them. The closest lipe_scan3 (doing the disk reading) gets to ldiskfs is:

  • memory pressure due to reading a lot of blocks and pushing kernel buffers, inodes, etc. out of cache (which seems to be the primary cause of the stack traces)
  • access a bunch of objects in userspace to get fid2path info, which might be accessing a strange broken/ancient file layout that make the inodes strange?

Yes. It looks like the bad address is the same for all three crashes:

BUG: unable to handle kernel paging request at 0000100000000064
Is this your file system you've upgraded since the beginning of time?
